central_banks.fed: The Federal Reserve held rates steady at 4.25-4.50% at its June meeting. Chair Powell noted inflation remains elevated but the labor market is cooling. Markets are pricing in one rate cut by September.
central_banks.boc: The Bank of Canada held its overnight rate at 3.25% in June. Governor Macklem highlighted that while core inflation is trending toward the 2% target, shelter costs remain sticky.

geopolitical.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
geopolitical.summary: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

geopolitical.title: Deadly Sudan drone strike targets funeral procession
geopolitical.summary: Rights groups accuse the RSF paramilitary group of killing civilians in el-Obeid, a city on the civil war's front line.

geopolitical.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing, and protests
geopolitical.summary: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

geopolitical.title: Nigeria evacuates citizens from South Africa as anti-migrant sentiment rises
geopolitical.summary: Nigeria is the latest African state to repatriate citizens following reports of xenophobic attacks.

geopolitical.title: The furious dispute over what caused Air India flight 171 to crash
geopolitical.summary: The final conclusions of the investigation have yet to be published, although more could become apparent in the coming days.

geopolitical.title: Vance says Israeli PM Netanyahu 'has got some things wrong'
geopolitical.summary: The US vice-president says the Israeli leader "aggressively asserts" his country's interests, but they do not always align with those of the US.

geopolitical.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Again Claims Deal Is Close After Retracting Threat of Strikes
geopolitical.summary: Claiming there was progress in peace negotiations, President Trump said he had canceled the next wave of planned attacks after two days of U.S. airstrikes.

geopolitical.title: U.K. Defense Secretary Resigns, in a Blow to Starmer
geopolitical.summary: John Healey unexpectedly announced that he was stepping down, saying Prime Minister Keir Starmer was failing to invest enough money in the British military.

geopolitical.title: This Is Mexico and Canada’s World Cup, Too. Don’t Expect Unity.
geopolitical.summary: Mexico and Canada have faced significant recent tension in their relations with the United States. But in their historic World Cup bid, the three countries promoted teamwork.

geopolitical.title: Women Who Fled Iran Are to Be Deported to Central African Republic, Lawyers Say
geopolitical.summary: The women are among nearly two dozen people slated to be sent to a country where the U.S. government has advised “Do not travel for any reason.”
